slices.focus: Shift from broad outreach to concentrated case tracking
slices.summary: Persuaded supervisor Sharon with cluster maps and deed records to narrow from corridor-wide clinics to concentrated tracking in a repeat-loss subsection. Building a milestone-based tracker with owner column to follow households from workshop attendance through signed scope. Grappling with depth-versus-coverage tradeoffs and the discomfort of measurable accountability that comes with visibility into outcomes rather than just activity counts.
open_loops: Whether concentrated focus will actually move more households to signed scope, or just feel more orderly
How to avoid making the rest of the corridor feel neglected while focusing deeply on one zone
Whether the tracker can hold facts without absorbing personal anxiety and over-identification
decisions: Narrow work scope to repeat-loss subsection after proving efficiency with cluster map and deed records
Build milestone-based tracking system prioritizing clarity over completeness, with a designated owner per file
Establish weekly review rhythm and defined cooling-off rules to avoid emotional file triage
